title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text:
Digital art has transformed the creative landscape, offering artists new tools and possibilities that were unimaginable just a few decades (0). . Using computers, tablets and specialised software, artists can now create stunning works that blur the line between traditional and technological expression.

When digital art first (1). , many critics dismissed it as inferior to traditional forms. They argued that working on a computer (2). away from the authenticity of painting or sculpture. However, attitudes have gradually (3). as the artistic quality and complexity of digital works have improved dramatically.

Today, digital art has (4). widespread recognition in galleries and museums worldwide. Major institutions now (5). exhibitions dedicated entirely to digital creations. The art market has also embraced this medium, with some digital pieces (6). for millions of dollars. Young artists are particularly (7). to digital tools, finding them more accessible and affordable than traditional materials. As technology continues to advance, the (8). between digital and physical art will likely become even less distinct.

example.explanation: 'Ago' is used to indicate a time in the past from the present.

questions.explanation: 'Emerge' is the correct verb to describe when something new emerges or appears.
'Take away from' significa restar valor o quitar importancia a algo.
'Shift' describes a gradual change of opinion or attitude.
'Gain recognition' is the correct placement to gain recognition.
'Hold exhibitions' is the correct placement for organizing exhibitions.
'Sell for' indicates the selling price of something.
'Be drawn to' means to be attracted to something.
'Boundary' is the correct noun to describe the dividing line between concepts.
